column4: Description

Soviet-era Fragmentation Grenade. 
Looks a lot like a lemon. It's pay-load is the least effective, but it gets the job done with one good toss.

Commonly found Post-WWII Soviet Hand Grenade. 
A simple yet effective design that is still in use today by many middle-eastern nations.

Jerry-rigged Hand Grenade using a VOG-25 Grenade with a removed warhead.
Extremely Short-Fuse. Throw and forget.
Standard Issue Smoke Grenade, effective for crossing areas of dense gunfire under cover.
Standard Issue Flashbang, capable of blinding and deafening a number of individuals within it's radius.

Standard Issue American Frag Grenade.
Highly Effective and Highly dangerous as a result of it's shrapnel.
Description

Polymer Magazine for use in the AK74 and resulting compatible platforms.
Comes in either Matte Black or Plum.

30 Round Polymer STANAG Mag for 5.56 systems.
Extremely light, and allows for ease of loading.
Company Produced 33 Round Glock Mag. Can be used for respective Vector Platforms.
Company Produced 30 Round Glock Mag. Can be used for respective Vector Platforms.
Extended Mag for the MP7A1 and A2.

Polymer Extended Magazine for the AK74 and resulting platforms.
Extends bullets in the mag by 15.

30 Round Polymer STANAG Mag for 5.56 systems. 
It's stripped design on the magazine ensures a stable grip on it's body whilst loading it into the magwell.

MaxRound Powermag 20-Round Mag for the Saiga 12.
Exceedingly Powerful, but can misfeed.

Polymer Stacked Magazine for the AK74 and resulting Platforms. 
Design extends the mag by a whole 30, but will weigh down the user as a whole.

Metal Mag for the AK103 or any resulting platforms. 
It's weight acts as a balancing point, letting the fire-arm feel stable towards the user.

Polymer Mag for the AK103 or any resulting platform. 
Lighter design allows for ease of use by the operator. 
Comes in Black or Banana Yellow (For whatever reason.)

60 Round Polymer STANAG Mag for 5.56 Systems
Upgrades AR-15 Platform rifles by a whole 30 Rounds, but prone to malfunctions.
Description
Large Toolkit, enabling the user to repair items or structures within his knowledge effectively.
Spray on Adhesive for repairing Ballistic Shields and Facemasks.

Identification Flare. Attach it your vest or bag for target recognition or to mark areas on the ground. 
Blue is generally associated with Friendly Forces

Identification Flare. Attach it your vest or bag for target recognition or to mark areas on the ground. 
Red signifies entry points, or areas of caution.

Identification Flare. Attach it your vest or bag for target recognition or to mark areas on the ground. 
Green is often utilized in low-visability enviroments.

Identification Flare. Attach it your vest or bag for target recognition or to mark areas on the ground. 
Yellow signifies 3rd Party Safety Nearby.

Military Purpose Green Laser Pointer for use in IR-Lasering Key Targets for Close Air Support.
Don't point it at people's eyes.

Multi-Purpose Breaching Tool for Doors and Hatches. 
Has another use, should you ever be attacked while holding it.

Special Operations Device for Thermal Marking Targets. 
It's primary use is to laser targets for third party intervention, but can double as a spotter scope.

Recon Drone, developed by Shironome R&D.
Highly Expensive, but works in tandem with CrossCom Glasses to provide accurate enemy location displays.
